entries.title: Final Exams Test Written Skill And Practical Judgment

context.label: Final exam week begins

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The end-of-term exam combines academic written tests with a practical exercise that tests judgment under pressure.
content.injection: During Final Exams, treat U.A. as testing more than combat strength: students must show judgment, cooperation, and preparation.
antiLore: Do not reduce the exam arc to only one fight against All Might.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Momo Becomes A Study Leader

context.label: Final exam week begins

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Momo helps classmates study, showing academic competence and a desire to be useful before her confidence is tested in combat.
content.injection: Before the practical exam, Momo can act as a serious study leader, while still carrying insecurity about live decision-making.
antiLore: Do not portray Momo as lacking intelligence or preparation.

entries.title: Teacher Practical Exams Are Tailored To Student Weaknesses

context.label: Teachers reveal practical exam twist

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The practical exam pairs students against teachers in matchups designed to pressure their specific weaknesses.
content.injection: Teacher exam matchups should feel deliberately instructional; U.A. is testing known flaws, not randomly arranging fights.
constraints: Teachers may use severe methods within exam constraints.
antiLore: Do not portray pairings as arbitrary tournament brackets.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Escape Is A Valid Exam Win Condition

context.label: Teachers reveal practical exam twist

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The practical exams can be passed by escaping or cuffing the teacher, so victory is not always direct defeat.
content.injection: In final exam scenes, students should weigh escape, teamwork, and teacher capture rather than assuming they must overpower a pro.
antiLore: Do not make every exam require defeating the teacher in raw combat.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Izuku And Bakugo Are Forced To Cooperate Against All Might

context.label: Izuku and Bakugo face All Might

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku and Bakugo are paired against All Might specifically to force their broken rivalry into practical cooperation.
content.injection: Their exam should be tense and dysfunctional: Izuku seeks strategy and escape, while Bakugo resists cooperation and wants to fight.
constraints: Their teamwork improves only under pressure.
antiLore: Do not portray them as comfortable partners yet.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: All Might Plays The Exam As An Overwhelming Wall

context.label: Izuku and Bakugo face All Might

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All Might functions as a near-overwhelming wall in the exam, forcing Izuku and Bakugo to confront the gap between student ambition and pro-level force.
content.injection: In the All Might practical exam, portray All Might as restrained but terrifyingly dominant compared to first-year students.
constraints: He is teaching, not trying to seriously injure them.
antiLore: Do not make All Might easy to overpower.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Bakugo Would Rather Fight Than Run

context.label: Izuku and Bakugo face All Might

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo’s pride makes him resist an escape-based strategy against All Might even when fighting directly is irrational.
content.injection: During the All Might exam, Bakugo should struggle to accept running as a valid tactical win.
antiLore: Do not make Bakugo immediately agree to cautious strategy.

entries.title: Izuku Carries Bakugo Toward The Exit

context.label: Izuku and Bakugo face All Might

content.fact: Izuku helps force the winning condition by carrying Bakugo toward escape despite their conflict.
content.injection: The exam resolution should preserve reluctant cooperation: Izuku acts decisively even while their relationship remains hostile.
antiLore: Do not make this a full friendship reconciliation.

entries.title: Yaoyorozu Must Regain Command Confidence

context.label: Yaoyorozu and Todoroki face Aizawa

content.fact: Momo’s exam with Todoroki tests her ability to trust her own planning under pressure after losing confidence from the Sports Festival.
content.injection: In Momo/Todoroki vs. Aizawa, center Momo’s recovery of decision-making authority rather than making Shoto solve the problem for her.
antiLore: Do not portray Momo as merely support equipment for Shoto.

entries.title: Todoroki Defers To Momo’s Plan

context.label: Yaoyorozu and Todoroki face Aizawa

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Todoroki deliberately supports Momo’s leadership during their practical exam, helping her rebuild confidence.
content.injection: Use Todoroki’s restraint in this exam as respect for Momo’s judgment, not as inability to act.
antiLore: Do not make Todoroki dominate the exam plan.

entries.title: Aizawa Tests Planning Under Quirk Suppression

context.label: Yaoyorozu and Todoroki face Aizawa

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aizawa’s Erasure pressures students who depend on Quirk output to use planning, timing, and environment.
content.injection: Against Aizawa, Quirk strength alone must not solve the exam; timing and deception matter because Erasure can shut abilities down.
antiLore: Do not let students ignore Erasure as a tactical constraint.

entries.title: Mineta Passes By Exploiting Midnight’s Weakness

context.label: Other final exam pairs resolve their weaknesses

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mineta passes the practical by exploiting Midnight’s weakness and using the exam rules despite his fear and poor motives.
content.injection: If referencing Mineta’s exam, focus on rule exploitation and fear management rather than making him generally heroic.
antiLore: Do not turn Mineta into a noble combat leader.

content.fact: Tokoyami and Tsuyu’s exam against Ectoplasm pressures them with clone numbers and a capture problem rather than a simple duel.
content.injection: Use Ectoplasm’s exam as a tactical numbers problem that rewards coordination and escape thinking.
antiLore: Do not make Ectoplasm a normal one-body opponent.

content.fact: Aoyama and Uraraka’s exam against Thirteen turns rescue-oriented power into a dangerous battlefield constraint.
content.injection: Against Thirteen, treat positioning and safety as central; Black Hole-like power makes reckless approach dangerous.

content.fact: Koda’s exam pressures his fear of insects and communication, forcing him to use Anivoice under stress.
content.injection: In Koda’s exam, his breakthrough should be quiet courage through communication rather than flashy combat aggression.
antiLore: Do not make Koda suddenly loud and brash as a personality change.

entries.title: Shigaraki Confronts Izuku In Public Without A Battle

context.label: Shigaraki confronts Izuku at the mall

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki confronts Izuku at the mall by threatening him in public, creating psychological pressure rather than an open fight.
content.injection: During the mall encounter, keep the danger quiet and social: Shigaraki’s hand on Izuku and the crowd around them prevent a normal battle.
antiLore: Do not turn the mall encounter into a public brawl.

entries.title: Shigaraki Begins Clarifying His Hatred Through Stain

context.label: Shigaraki confronts Izuku at the mall

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The mall conversation helps Shigaraki clarify that he hates All Might and the society built around him, not merely isolated targets.
content.injection: Post-mall Shigaraki should be more focused than post-USJ, but still immature compared with later arcs.
antiLore: Do not give Shigaraki his later fully evolved ideology yet.

entries.title: Summer Camp Setup Should Foreshadow Increased Training

context.label: Summer training camp is set up

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After final exams, the story moves toward summer training camp as U.A. escalates student development after recent villain pressure.
content.injection: At the end of this deck, summer camp should be framed as the next supervised training step, not yet the Vanguard Squad attack.
antiLore: Do not leak the training camp attack before that deck’s Context.

content.fact: Nezu’s exam design reflects strategic intelligence and a willingness to stress-test students with uncomfortable matchups.
content.injection: Use Nezu as institution-level intelligence behind the exam structure, not as a passive mascot principal.

content.fact: The final exam period happens after Hosu, so Stain’s effects on reputation, rumors, and Iida’s emotional state remain relevant.
content.injection: During Final Exams, Hosu must not vanish from continuity; Iida and public perception still carry its aftermath.
antiLore: Do not reset the cast emotionally to pre-Stain status.

entries.title: Bakugo And Izuku Still Lack Mutual Trust

context.label: Izuku and Bakugo face All Might

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Even after the Sports Festival and Stain, Bakugo and Izuku do not yet have mutual trust; the exam exposes that dysfunction.
content.injection: Their forced partnership should remain raw and hostile, with cooperation achieved under pressure rather than emotional resolution.
antiLore: Do not jump to their later understanding.

entries.title: Mall Civilians Make Shigaraki’s Threat Effective

context.label: Shigaraki confronts Izuku at the mall

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The mall crowd makes Shigaraki’s threat effective because Izuku cannot safely escalate without risking civilians.
content.injection: In the mall encounter, civilian density should restrain hero action and make the threat psychologically tight.
antiLore: Do not make Izuku freely fight Shigaraki in the mall.

entries.title: Police And Adults Do Not Fully Understand The Mall Talk

context.label: Shigaraki confronts Izuku at the mall

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The mall confrontation is an intimate threat that adults can respond to afterward but cannot fully control in the moment.
content.injection: After the mall encounter, adults may know Shigaraki approached Izuku, but the psychological content of the conversation remains mostly Izuku’s burden.
antiLore: Do not make the encounter an open official interrogation scene while it is happening.

entries.title: Forest Camp Attack Must Stay Future-Gated

context.label: Summer training camp is set up

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: At the end of Final Exams, the summer training camp is planned, but the villain attack and Vanguard Action Squad details are still future canon.
content.injection: Before the training camp deck Context, do not leak that villains will attack the camp or that Bakugo will be captured.
constraints: This card is a future guard for the next deck.
antiLore: Do not foreshadow the attack with specific names, routes, or capture outcomes in ordinary pre-camp scenes.
